title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston ranked as the most collision-prone U.S. city, with drivers averaging 3.7 years between crashes.
Worcester and Springfield, MA ranked among the bottom five, with over five years between collisions.
Brownsville, TX deemed safest city, with drivers averaging nearly 15 years between crashes.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, accused of leading a 105 mph police chase on the Bourne Bridge, was held without bail.
Police say the Falmouth man drove the wrong way during the chase, weaving through traffic and risking public safety.
Deleon faces multiple charges, including assault with a dangerous weapon, speeding, and resisting arrest, with a hearing set for Aug. 5.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police released new photos of a second juvenile involved in a South Boston lemonade stand robbery.
A 14-year-old boy was previously arrested for the June 10 armed robbery on West Ninth Street.
Police ask the public for information on the suspect, contact Boston Police District C-6 Detectives or submit tips anonymously.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is considering new NBA team options after leaving the Lakers, targeting a fifth championship.
Rich Paul mentioned Boston Celtics with Brad Stevens and Jayson Tatum as possible but unlikely landing spots for James.
Boston's potential lineup with James includes Derrick White, Paul George, and Mitchell Robinson, enhancing their contender status.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Celtics legend Robert Parish criticized the decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Parish compared the Brown trade to the Dallas Mavericks' controversial trade of Luka Doncic in 2025.
Parish suggested that if Red Auerbach were alive, he would have strongly disapproved of the trade.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum penned a farewell message to Jaylen Brown after his trade to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Jaylen Brown was traded for Paul George and multiple draft picks after nine seasons with the Celtics.
Brown expressed feeling disrespected by the Celtics organization during the trade process.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics center Neemias Queta agreed to a four-year, $56 million extension, keeping him with the team through 2030-31.
The Celtics picked up Queta's $2.67 million team option for 2026-27 and extended his contract for four additional years.
Queta averaged 10.2 points, 8.4 rebounds, and 1.3 blocks per game in the 2025-26 season, finishing fourth in Most Improved Player voting.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates 200 years as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
The building, designated a National Historic Landmark in 2003, dates back to 1716 or 1717.
The restaurant serves 1,000-1,500 guests daily, maintaining 40% of its original 1826 menu.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston hosts major events: Boston Pops Fireworks, World Cup quarterfinal, and Sail Boston Parade of Sail from July 4-11.
The Boston Pops Fireworks on July 4 includes performances by Chance the Rapper and a themed drone show.
Gillette Stadium hosts a World Cup quarterfinal on July 9, and Sail Boston begins July 11 with a tall ships parade.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House, Boston's oldest restaurant, has hosted six U.S. presidents including JFK and Obama.
Owner Joseph Milano commissioned a painting of over 50 famous patrons for a 2003 artwork by Stan Kotzen.
Celebrities who dined there include Al Pacino, Meryl Streep, Matt Damon, and Tiger Woods.
